definition: The Kalecki-Levy profit equation is the correct framework for understanding corporate earnings sustainability in this cycle. Fiscal consolidation >2pp GDP would mechanically reduce aggregate profits, triggering interest coverage deterioration and credit cycle acceleration within 4-6 quarters.
evidence: Fiscal consolidation triggers profit decline → coverage deterioration → credit cycle acceleration within 4-6 quarters
pair_2 debate judged this a decisive win over Solow/operating-leverage frameworks for this cycle
The 2pp threshold is a heuristic — behavioral pass-through elasticities are imprecisely estimated
open_questions: What is the precise fiscal consolidation threshold?
How elastic is the behavioral pass-through from fiscal to profits?
Can the framework predict the timing of credit cycle turns?
